reason: Both answers correctly reject microservices, correctly diagnose the CI/CD pipeline as the real bottleneck, cite the same Shopify/GitHub precedent, and recommend modular monolith (Packwerk) as a fallback if coupling becomes painful — hitting all four key points. They are structurally and substantively nearly identical (same fixes: merge queue/continuous deploy, faster CI via Knapsack Pro, feature flags, canary/rollback). Answer A has one extra bullet (explicit merge-queue tooling) while Answer B adds a concrete CI time target (<10 min) — these are minor, roughly offsetting differences in specificity, not meaningful quality gaps. No factual errors in either. This is a genuine tie.
